Top-nav button variations

Four directions for the </> + ⓘ + ⚙ cluster. All use SVG icons (consistent rendering across glyphs) at the same visual weight. Each variation is shown inside a fake header bar so you can judge it in context. Hover the buttons to see the interactive states.

V1 · Ghost (borderless)

Pure icons, no borders. Hover reveals a soft background. Code View button uses the accent color so it stays visually distinct without needing a separator. Most minimal — lets the title breathe.

Claude Advisor Tool Playground v1.5.0

V2 · Pill cluster (segmented)

All three buttons live inside a single rounded container with hairline dividers between them. Reads as a unified "utility bar". Compact and tidy. Code View still gets the accent color but sits inside the same shell.

Claude Advisor Tool Playground v1.5.0

V3 · Tinted Code button + ghost icons

Code View has a subtle blue-tinted background and border so it draws the eye. About and Settings stay quiet as ghost icons. Strongest visual hierarchy — Code View clearly the "new feature" without shouting.

Claude Advisor Tool Playground v1.5.0

V4 · Labeled Code button + ghost icons

Code View becomes an explicit labeled pill (icon + "Code" text). About and Settings stay quiet as ghost icons. Most discoverable for new users — they don't need to hover to learn what it does. Slightly busier than the others.

Claude Advisor Tool Playground v1.5.0

What changed across all four: swapped the Unicode characters (ⓘ, ⚙, </>) for outline SVG icons. SVGs are stroke-based and render at consistent visual weight regardless of glyph — no more "ⓘ looks bigger than ⚙ at the same font-size".

My recommendation: V3 (tinted Code button + ghost icons). Code View gets the visual prominence it deserves as a new feature, About and Settings recede appropriately. V1 is a strong second if you want maximum minimalism. V2 is tidy but a bit "toolbar-heavy" for just three items. V4 is best for discoverability but loses the icon-grid feel.
